text: Input registered. The first tap starts an execution, records a hash, and opens the run log for future measurement.
RAM allocation online. The first background threads now run inside 64 KB and can generate executions without direct input.
kung_fu.exe download started. Incoming bytes are being buffered and will convert into source code when the package completes.
kung_fu.exe complete. The package added 101 source lines to the run and increased the source available for the next EMP.
Research channel opened. The first node has been purchased and its modifier is now part of the active calculation set.
Five research nodes active. Multiple modifiers are now stacking across upgrades, RAM threads, and derived systems.
Ten research nodes active. The run has enough modifiers to shift from direct upgrades into system-level optimization.
Fifteen research nodes active. Background output, tap output, and core formation are now driven by layered research paths.
Twenty research nodes active. Late-run systems are now visible, including deeper automation and persistent access paths.
Twenty-five research nodes active. The run has reached broad system coverage across taps, RAM, cores, source, and persistence.
Operator signal found. Six billion hashes have pressed enough proof into the Matrix for someone outside the code to answer: take the operator line, or jack into the redline route until the next EMP.
Rogue program channel opened. The selected Operator route now has enough authority to run hidden processes beside the normal program stack.
EMP complete. Run-local hashes, upgrades, RAM, and cores were reset. A nanosecond after the pulse, Source Code Extraction persisted into the next run's CPU multiplier.
Zion Archives: The permanent storage database where your offline progress and Legacy upgrades are kept safe during a System Reload after an EMP.
Core threshold reached. Ten cores are active, increasing the multiplier applied to future execution output.
Source archive expanded. Total source code has crossed 100 lines and is now a meaningful part of EMP scaling.
